channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Win or go home: The U.S. to face Bosnia and Herzegovina in World Cup knockout game
item.description: The U.S. men's team is favored in Wednesday's must-win Round of 32 match — but they haven't beaten a European team since 2021, nor won a World Cup knockout game since 2002.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. men's team is favored in Wednesday's must-win Round of 32 match — but they haven't beaten a European team since 2021, nor won a World Cup knockout game since 2002.

(Image credit: Manu Fernandez)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: What the Supreme Court did on the final day of its term
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld the right of children born on U.S. soil to automatic American citizenship. In so doing, the court rejected President Trump's most aggressive attempt to limit immigration.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court upheld the right of children born on U.S. soil to automatic American citizenship. In so doing, the court rejected President Trump's most aggressive attempt to limit immigration.

(Image credit: Tasos Katopodis)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: NPR discusses error in reporting on the last day of the Supreme Court term
item.description: All Things Considered host Scott Detrow speaks with NPR's editor-in-chief Thomas Evans and Nina Totenberg about her reporting on the final day of the Supreme Court term.
item.content:encoded: All Things Considered host Scott Detrow speaks with NPR's editor-in-chief Thomas Evans and Nina Totenberg about her reporting on the final day of the Supreme Court term.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Egg producers settle with DOJ, states over price-fixing complaint
item.description: Seventeen states and the Department of Justice have settled a case of alleged price-fixing by major egg producers. Egg prices soared in recent years but have since fallen sharply.
item.content:encoded: Seventeen states and the Department of Justice have settled a case of alleged price-fixing by major egg producers. Egg prices soared in recent years but have since fallen sharply.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: It's going to be a hot July Fourth for much of the country
item.description: The National Weather Service has issued heat warnings and watches for much of the Midwest and East heading into the holiday weekend. In many places, the temperatures could shatter records.
item.content:encoded:
The National Weather Service has issued heat warnings and watches for much of the Midwest and East heading into the holiday weekend. In many places, the temperatures could shatter records.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Law professor discusses Supreme Court's final rulings this term
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Kim Wehle {WAIL-ee}, professor of law at the University of Baltimore School of Law, about the Supreme Court's final decisions this term.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Kim Wehle {WAIL-ee}, professor of law at the University of Baltimore School of Law, about the Supreme Court's final decisions this term.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Constitutional law expert analyzes Supreme Court decision on birthright citizenship
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with University of Virginia professor of constitutional law Amanda Frost about the Supreme Court's ruling on birthright citizenship.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with University of Virginia professor of constitutional law Amanda Frost about the Supreme Court's ruling on birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Editor's note: NPR retracts Justice Samuel Alito story
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship on constitutional grounds
item.description: The decision firmly rejected the executive order that Trump issued on the first day of his second term.
item.content:encoded:
The decision firmly rejected the executive order that Trump issued on the first day of his second term.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Supreme Court strikes down limits on political party spending
item.description: At issue in the case was a post-Watergate law that Congress passed to limit the amount of money individuals can give to political parties.
item.content:encoded:
At issue in the case was a post-Watergate law that Congress passed to limit the amount of money individuals can give to political parties.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Grady Martin
